main_paper.raw: {"methods":["Context-Aware BERT for Knowledge Graph Completion (CAB-KGC), which integrates contextual information from linked entities and relations into BERT for tail entity prediction","Elimination of entity descriptions and negative triplet sampling in the model design","Use of cross-entropy loss for training instead of negative sampling","Introduction of Evaluation based on Distance from Average Solution (EDAS) as an evaluation criterion for KGC"],"problems":["Knowledge graph completion (KGC), specifically tail entity prediction in incomplete knowledge graphs","Generalization to unseen entities and relationships during testing","High computational overhead, semantic inconsistencies, and data imbalance due to negative triplet sampling in textual-based models","Dependence on entity descriptions, which are often unavailable, in BERT-based KGC models","Lack of utilization of structural/contextual information from the knowledge graph in existing methods","Inconsistencies and limitations in current evaluation metrics (MR, MRR, Hit@K) for KGC"],"datasets":["FB15k-237","WN18RR","CoDEx-S","ConceptNet100K"],"metrics":["Mean Reciprocal Rank (MRR)","Hit@1","Hit@3","Mean Rank (MR)","Evaluation based on Distance from Average Solution (EDAS)"],"results":[{"metric":"Hit@1 (WN18RR)","value":"6.88% improvement over SOTA"},{"metric":"Hit@1 (CoDEx-S)","value":"14.32% improvement over SOTA"},{"metric":"Hit@1 (ConceptNet100K)","value":"17.13% improvement over SOTA"},{"metric":"EDAS ranking","value":"CAB-KGC ranked as top-performing model"}],"novelty_claims":["CAB-KGC uniquely leverages contextual information from head entities and relations (structural KG features) for tail prediction, which prior methods overlook.","CAB-KGC eliminates the need for entity descriptions, enabling use on datasets with sparse or missing descriptive information.","CAB-KGC avoids negative triplet sampling by using cross-entropy loss, improving training speed and robustness.","The paper introduces the EDAS evaluation criterion to the KG domain, providing a more comprehensive and reliable comparative framework for KGC models.","CAB-KGC achieves state-of-the-art performance on multiple benchmark datasets, outperforming existing methods."]}

methods: Context-Aware BERT for Knowledge Graph Completion (CAB-KGC), which integrates contextual information from linked entities and relations into BERT for tail entity prediction
Elimination of entity descriptions and negative triplet sampling in the model design
Use of cross-entropy loss for training instead of negative sampling
Introduction of Evaluation based on Distance from Average Solution (EDAS) as an evaluation criterion for KGC
problems: Knowledge graph completion (KGC), specifically tail entity prediction in incomplete knowledge graphs
Generalization to unseen entities and relationships during testing
High computational overhead, semantic inconsistencies, and data imbalance due to negative triplet sampling in textual-based models
Dependence on entity descriptions, which are often unavailable, in BERT-based KGC models
Lack of utilization of structural/contextual information from the knowledge graph in existing methods
Inconsistencies and limitations in current evaluation metrics (MR, MRR, Hit@K) for KGC
metrics: Evaluation based on Distance from Average Solution (EDAS)
novelty_claims: CAB-KGC uniquely leverages contextual information from head entities and relations (structural KG features) for tail prediction, which prior methods overlook.
CAB-KGC eliminates the need for entity descriptions, enabling use on datasets with sparse or missing descriptive information.
CAB-KGC avoids negative triplet sampling by using cross-entropy loss, improving training speed and robustness.
The paper introduces the EDAS evaluation criterion to the KG domain, providing a more comprehensive and reliable comparative framework for KGC models.
CAB-KGC achieves state-of-the-art performance on multiple benchmark datasets, outperforming existing methods.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["KICGPT framework: integrates a large language model (LLM) with a triple-based KGC retriever for knowledge graph completion","Knowledge Prompt: a novel in-context learning strategy that encodes structural knowledge into demonstrations to guide the LLM for re-ranking candidate entities","Retriever-based candidate generation: a structure-aware KG model generates an ordered candidate entity list for each query","LLM-based re-ranking: the LLM re-ranks the top-m entities from the retriever's candidate list using in-context learning prompts"],"problems":["Knowledge graph completion (KGC), specifically the link prediction task","Addressing the long-tail entity problem in KGC, where entities with few connections are hard to predict due to information scarcity","Reducing the training and finetuning overhead required by text-based KGC methods","Constraining LLM outputs to valid KG entities and overcoming input length limitations for LLMs in KGC tasks"],"datasets":["(Not explicitly listed in the provided content, but referred to as 'benchmark datasets')"],"metrics":["(Not explicitly listed in the provided content, but standard KGC metrics are likely: Mean Reciprocal Rank (MRR), Hits@k, etc.)"],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["KICGPT is the first framework to combine large language models (LLMs) with triple-based KGC methods for knowledge graph completion","Proposes a novel in-context learning strategy, Knowledge Prompt, specifically designed for KGC tasks","KICGPT achieves state-of-the-art performance on benchmark datasets with lower training overhead and no finetuning required for the LLM","KICGPT enables simultaneous utilization and alignment of knowledge from both the KG and the LLM's internal knowledge base, alleviating information scarcity for long-tail entities","KICGPT constrains LLM outputs by formalizing link prediction as a re-ranking task, addressing the unconstrained output and input length limitations of LLMs in KGC"]}

methods: KICGPT framework: integrates a large language model (LLM) with a triple-based KGC retriever for knowledge graph completion
Knowledge Prompt: a novel in-context learning strategy that encodes structural knowledge into demonstrations to guide the LLM for re-ranking candidate entities
Retriever-based candidate generation: a structure-aware KG model generates an ordered candidate entity list for each query
LLM-based re-ranking: the LLM re-ranks the top-m entities from the retriever's candidate list using in-context learning prompts
problems: Knowledge graph completion (KGC), specifically the link prediction task
Addressing the long-tail entity problem in KGC, where entities with few connections are hard to predict due to information scarcity
Reducing the training and finetuning overhead required by text-based KGC methods
Constraining LLM outputs to valid KG entities and overcoming input length limitations for LLMs in KGC tasks
datasets: (Not explicitly listed in the provided content, but referred to as 'benchmark datasets')
metrics: (Not explicitly listed in the provided content, but standard KGC metrics are likely: Mean Reciprocal Rank (MRR), Hits@k, etc.)
novelty_claims: KICGPT is the first framework to combine large language models (LLMs) with triple-based KGC methods for knowledge graph completion
Proposes a novel in-context learning strategy, Knowledge Prompt, specifically designed for KGC tasks
KICGPT achieves state-of-the-art performance on benchmark datasets with lower training overhead and no finetuning required for the LLM
KICGPT enables simultaneous utilization and alignment of knowledge from both the KG and the LLM's internal knowledge base, alleviating information scarcity for long-tail entities
KICGPT constrains LLM outputs by formalizing link prediction as a re-ranking task, addressing the unconstrained output and input length limitations of LLMs in KGC

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["MPIKGC framework: a general framework that leverages large language models (LLMs) to improve knowledge graph completion (KGC) from multiple perspectives","Entity description expansion using LLMs with Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting to generate richer entity descriptions","Relation understanding enhancement via three curated LLM prompting strategies: global, local, and reverse prompts","Structural enrichment by querying LLMs to extract additional structural information, summarize keywords, measure entity similarity, and create new triples"],"problems":["Incompleteness of knowledge graphs (KGs)","Limited quality and coverage of entity descriptions in description-based KGC","Ambiguity in relation understanding due to reliance on relation names","Sparse graph structure, especially for long-tailed entities, leading to sub-optimal KGC performance"],"datasets":["Freebase","DBpedia","YAGO","(Fourth dataset not explicitly named in the provided content)"],"metrics":["Link prediction accuracy","Triplet classification accuracy"],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["Proposes MPIKGC, a general framework that systematically leverages LLMs to improve KGC from multiple perspectives: entity description, relation understanding, and structural enrichment","Introduces Chain-of-Thought prompting for entity description expansion in KGC","Designs three specific LLM prompting strategies (global, local, reverse) for enhanced relation understanding","Utilizes LLMs to extract and generate new structural information for KGs, addressing sparsity and long-tail issues","Demonstrates the generalizability and effectiveness of the framework across multiple KGC models, datasets, and LLMs"]}

methods: MPIKGC framework: a general framework that leverages large language models (LLMs) to improve knowledge graph completion (KGC) from multiple perspectives
Entity description expansion using LLMs with Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting to generate richer entity descriptions
Relation understanding enhancement via three curated LLM prompting strategies: global, local, and reverse prompts
Structural enrichment by querying LLMs to extract additional structural information, summarize keywords, measure entity similarity, and create new triples
problems: Incompleteness of knowledge graphs (KGs)
Limited quality and coverage of entity descriptions in description-based KGC
Ambiguity in relation understanding due to reliance on relation names
Sparse graph structure, especially for long-tailed entities, leading to sub-optimal KGC performance
datasets: (Fourth dataset not explicitly named in the provided content)
novelty_claims: Proposes MPIKGC, a general framework that systematically leverages LLMs to improve KGC from multiple perspectives: entity description, relation understanding, and structural enrichment
Introduces Chain-of-Thought prompting for entity description expansion in KGC
Designs three specific LLM prompting strategies (global, local, reverse) for enhanced relation understanding
Utilizes LLMs to extract and generate new structural information for KGs, addressing sparsity and long-tail issues
Demonstrates the generalizability and effectiveness of the framework across multiple KGC models, datasets, and LLMs

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["CSProm-KG (Conditional Soft Prompts for KGC): a structure-aware frozen PLM approach using Conditional Soft Prompts generated from entity and relation embeddings to fuse structural and textual knowledge for KGC","Local Adversarial Regularization: a technique to help distinguish textually similar entities in the KG","Ablation studies to analyze the effectiveness of prompt-based methods and individual components"],"problems":["Knowledge Graph Completion (KGC): inferring missing facts in knowledge graphs","Balancing structural and textual information in KGC models, especially in PLM-based approaches that tend to overemphasize textual information and overlook graph structure"],"datasets":["WN18RR","FB15K-237","Wikidata5M","ICEWS14","ICEWS05-15"],"metrics":["MRR (Mean Reciprocal Rank)","Hits@1","Hits@3","Hits@10"],"results":[{"metric":"MRR (WN18RR)","value":"CSProm-KG sets new state-of-the-art (exact value not provided)"},{"metric":"MRR (FB15K-237)","value":"CSProm-KG sets new state-of-the-art (exact value not provided)"},{"metric":"MRR (Wikidata5M)","value":"CSProm-KG sets new state-of-the-art (exact value not provided)"},{"metric":"MRR (ICEWS14)","value":"CSProm-KG sets new state-of-the-art (exact value not provided)"},{"metric":"MRR (ICEWS05-15)","value":"CSProm-KG sets new state-of-the-art (exact value not provided)"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes Conditional Soft Prompts for KGC (CSProm-KG), which condition prompt vectors on entity and relation embeddings to fuse structural and textual knowledge in frozen PLMs for KGC","Introduces Local Adversarial Regularization to help distinguish textually similar entities in KGs","Demonstrates that prompt-based methods can outperform fine-tuning approaches for KGC, achieving new state-of-the-art results on multiple static and temporal KGC benchmarks","Shows the flexibility and efficiency of CSProm-KG across different graph-based models"]}

methods: CSProm-KG (Conditional Soft Prompts for KGC): a structure-aware frozen PLM approach using Conditional Soft Prompts generated from entity and relation embeddings to fuse structural and textual knowledge for KGC

(MR)","Inference Time"],"results":[{"metric":"Mean Rank (MR) on FB15k-237","value":"51 (StAR) vs. 95 (KG-BERT) vs. 3340 (RotatE)"},{"metric":"Inference Time on FB15k-237","value":"6.5 hours (StAR) vs. 30 days (KG-BERT)"},{"metric":"Hits@N","value":"StAR achieves higher Hits@N than KG-BERT and RotatE, especially for small N"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes a hybrid model that augments textual encoding methods: Hybrid model combining textual encoding and graph embedding paradigms
Siamese-style textual encoder for partitioned triple representation
Structure learning via spatial measurement (translation function) and contrastive objective
Deterministic binary neural classifier for triple plausibility
Self-adaptive ensemble scheme incorporating scores from an existing graph embedding model (e.g., RotatE)
problems: Knowledge graph completion (KGC), specifically link prediction
Generalization to unseen entities/relations in KGs
High inference overheads in textual encoding approaches due to combinatorial explosion
Lack of structured knowledge in textual encoders leading to entity ambiguity
Vulnerability of graph embedding approaches to graph incompleteness
datasets: Three benchmarks (not all named in the excerpt)
A zero-shot dataset for link prediction

results.value: StAR achieves higher Hits@N than KG-BERT and RotatE, especially for small N

novelty_claims: Proposes a hybrid model that augments textual encoding with graph embedding techniques to combine contextualized and structured knowledge for KGC.
Introduces a Siamese-style textual encoder that partitions triples and encodes them for better generalization and structure learning.
Employs a structure learning component using spatial measurement and contrastive objective to enhance entity disambiguation and efficiency.
Develops a self-adaptive ensemble scheme that merges outputs from the proposed model and an existing graph embedding model to further boost performance.
Achieves state-of-the-art results on multiple benchmarks and demonstrates significant reduction in inference time compared to previous textual encoding methods.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["SimKGC: a simple contrastive knowledge graph completion method using pre-trained language models","Use of three types of negatives: in-batch negatives, pre-batch negatives, and self-negatives (hard negatives) for contrastive learning","Adoption of bi-encoder architecture to increase in-batch negatives","Caching vectors from previous batches as pre-batch negatives","Mining self-negatives (head entity as hard negative)","Switching loss function from margin-based ranking loss to InfoNCE loss","Simple re-ranking strategy by boosting scores of head entity’s k-hop neighbors"],"problems":["Inefficiency of contrastive learning in text-based knowledge graph completion methods","Performance gap between text-based and embedding-based KGC methods","Inductive knowledge graph completion (handling unseen entities)"],"datasets":["WN18RR","FB15k-237","Wikidata5M (transductive setting)","Wikidata5M (inductive setting)"],"metrics":["Mean Reciprocal Rank (MRR)","Hits@1","Hits@3","Hits@10"],"results":[{"metric":"MRR (WN18RR)","value":"47.6 → 66.6"},{"metric":"MRR (Wikidata5M, transductive)","value":"29.0 → 35.8"},{"metric":"MRR (Wikidata5M, inductive)","value":"49.3 → 71.4"}],"novelty_claims":["Introduction of three types of negatives (in-batch, pre-batch, self-negatives) for efficient contrastive learning in text-based KGC","Use of InfoNCE loss instead of margin-based ranking loss to focus on hard negatives","Demonstration that text-based KGC methods can outperform embedding-based methods with efficient contrastive learning","Simple re-ranking strategy leveraging k-hop neighbors to improve performance","Substantial improvement over state-of-the-art on multiple KGC benchmarks, especially in inductive settings"]}

methods: SimKGC: a simple contrastive knowledge graph completion method using pre-trained language models

Adoption of bi-encoder architecture to increase in-batch negatives
Caching vectors from previous batches as pre-batch negatives
Mining self-negatives (head entity as hard negative)
Switching loss function from margin-based ranking loss to InfoNCE loss
Simple re-ranking strategy by boosting scores of head entity’s k-hop neighbors
problems: Inefficiency of contrastive learning in text-based knowledge graph completion methods

Inductive knowledge graph completion (handling unseen entities)
novelty_claims: Introduction of three types of negatives (in-batch, pre-batch, self-negatives) for efficient contrastive learning in text-based KGC

Demonstration that text-based KGC methods can outperform embedding-based methods with efficient contrastive learning

Substantial improvement over state-of-the-art on multiple KGC benchmarks, especially in inductive settings

methods: A general and transferable framework for Temporal Knowledge Graph Completion (TKGC) using Large Language Models (LLMs)
Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) of LLMs for TKGC
Incorporation of local information from multiple single-step graphs for historical data augmentation
Incorporation of neighboring interaction information of entities and relations
Mitigation of the 'reversal curse' in structured expression reasoning via reverse data incorporation
Partitioning known data into input and supervised labeling segments for finetuning
problems: Temporal Knowledge Graph Completion (TKGC): predicting missing event links at future timestamps
Effectively leveraging LLMs for reasoning over temporal knowledge graphs
Mitigating the reversal curse in structured knowledge reasoning with LLMs
Improving LLMs' ability to perceive and utilize graph-structural information in TKGs

results.value: Framework exceeds or parallels existing methods across numerous popular metrics (exact values not specified in provided text)
Reported for several open-source models as comparative reference (exact values not specified in provided text)

novelty_claims: First comprehensive framework leveraging LLMs for temporal knowledge graph completion with a focus on both structural and textual reasoning.
Proposes a parameter-efficient fine-tuning strategy (PEFT) to adapt LLMs for TKGC tasks.
Introduces historical data augmentation using local information from multiple single-step graphs to enhance LLMs' structural awareness.
Systematically explores and mitigates the reversal curse in LLM-based structured knowledge reasoning via reverse data incorporation.
Provides extensive ablation studies and comparisons with advanced commercial LLMs (e.g., GPT-4, GPT-3.5-turbo) to analyze key factors influencing LLM performance on TKGC.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Proposing a new evaluation setting for KGC based on the open-world assumption (OWA) with human annotation for unknown triples","Introducing PKGC, a novel PLM-based KGC model that converts triples and support information into natural prompt sentences using prompt templates and soft prompts, leveraging prompt tuning"],"problems":["Inaccurate evaluation of PLM-based KGC models due to the closed-world assumption (CWA) underestimating their performance","Inappropriate utilization of PLMs in KGC models by simply splicing entity and relation labels, resulting in incoherent inputs and underutilization of PLM knowledge"],"datasets":["Wikidata (sampled subset)","Freebase (sampled subset)"],"metrics":["Hits@10","Link prediction accuracy"],"results":[{"metric":"Hits@10 (PLM-based KGC vs SOTA KGE-based model under CWA)","value":"about 20.8% lower for PLM-based KGC"},{"metric":"Effectiveness of PKGC under OWA","value":"PKGC outperforms previous PLM-based KGC models and is less sensitive to training data size"}],"novelty_claims":["First to highlight and systematically evaluate KGC under the open-world assumption (OWA) with human annotation for unknown triples, providing a more reliable evaluation for PLM-based KGC models","Proposes PKGC, a novel PLM-based KGC model that uses prompt-based input construction (natural prompt sentences and soft prompts) to better leverage PLM knowledge for KGC","Demonstrates that OWA is a more accurate evaluation setting for KGC, especially for PLM-based models and link prediction tasks","Shows that prompt-based utilization of PLMs enables better reasoning and knowledge combination from both PLMs and KGs"]}

methods: Proposing a new evaluation setting for KGC based on the open-world assumption (OWA) with human annotation for unknown triples
Introducing PKGC, a novel PLM-based KGC model that converts triples and support information into natural prompt sentences using prompt templates and soft prompts, leveraging prompt tuning
problems: Inaccurate evaluation of PLM-based KGC models due to the closed-world assumption (CWA) underestimating their performance
Inappropriate utilization of PLMs in KGC models by simply splicing entity and relation labels, resulting in incoherent inputs and underutilization of PLM knowledge

results.metric: Hits@10 (PLM-based KGC vs SOTA KGE-based model under CWA)

results.value: PKGC outperforms previous PLM-based KGC models and is less sensitive to training data size

novelty_claims: First to highlight and systematically evaluate KGC under the open-world assumption (OWA) with human annotation for unknown triples, providing a more reliable evaluation for PLM-based KGC models
Proposes PKGC, a novel PLM-based KGC model that uses prompt-based input construction (natural prompt sentences and soft prompts) to better leverage PLM knowledge for KGC
Demonstrates that OWA is a more accurate evaluation setting for KGC, especially for PLM-based models and link prediction tasks
Shows that prompt-based utilization of PLMs enables better reasoning and knowledge combination from both PLMs and KGs

methods: Knowledge Graph LLM (KG-LLM): a framework that models knowledge graph triples as text sequences and uses entity and relation descriptions as prompts for LLMs to predict triple plausibility
Instruction tuning of open LLMs (LLaMA, ChatGLM) on knowledge graph completion tasks, treating the problem as a sequence-to-sequence task
problems: Knowledge graph incompleteness
Knowledge graph completion: evaluating the plausibility of missing triples in a knowledge graph

results.metric: Performance of fine-tuned LLaMA-7B/ChatGLM-6B vs. ChatGPT/GPT-4
results.value: Fine-tuned smaller models outperform ChatGPT and GPT-4

novelty_claims: First systematic investigation of large language models for knowledge graph completion tasks

Demonstrates that fine-tuned smaller open LLMs can outperform proprietary large models like ChatGPT and GPT-4 on KG completion tasks

methods: DIFT: A finetuning framework for LLMs using discrimination instructions for KG completion
Use of a lightweight embedding-based model to generate candidate entities for each incomplete fact
Parameter-efficient finetuning of LLMs (e.g., with LoRA) to select the correct entity from candidates
Truncated sampling method to select useful KG samples for instruction construction, reducing finetuning cost
Injection of KG embeddings into LLMs to enhance reasoning ability

High computational cost of finetuning LLMs for KG completion
Inefficiency and errors in mapping LLM outputs to KG entities
novelty_claims: Proposes DIFT, a new framework that finetunes generative LLMs with discrimination instructions for KG completion, avoiding the need to ground LLM outputs to KG entities

Injects KG embeddings into LLMs to further enhance finetuning effectiveness
Demonstrates state-of-the-art KG completion results on benchmark datasets
